1.content:
Arcanthea is the name of this PLANET.  Industrialization and steampunk style machinery co-exist with ancient magic. The planet is dominated by vast oceans with two main continents - Orin, the bigger of the two, and Drachenland. All kinds of fantasy races inhabit the world, along with common fantasy monsters.

The starting year is 1865 Common Era.
1.name: Arcanthea

2.content:
Arcanthea has three moons, observed as celestial bodies through telescopes:
 - Chronos: The largest moon, silvery white; Associated with time, fate, and prophecy.
 - Mystra: Medium-sized moon with a slight blue tinge; Associated with arcane energy and spellcraft.
 - Animus: The smallest moon, with a warm golden glow; Associated with life, death, and spiritual energy.
2.name: Moons

3.content:
Other notable celestial bodies visible from Arcanthea:
 - The Twins (binary stars): One red, one blue
 - Halcyon, also known as Merchant's Light: A bright yellowish planet, used for navigation
 - Moorindal: A distant ice planet visible only through telescopes
 - Kerlin, The Veiled Lady: A ringed gas giant with mysterious colorful bands
 - Aether: A mystical nebula that glows softly at night
 - Zyphyr: A comet that passes every decade, marking a time of great change or upheaval
3.name: Celestial Bodies

4.content:
Notable languages in Arcanthea:
 - Vaillan, also known as Common Orinian, or simply Common. Native language of Vaillancourt and lingua franca in Orin.
 - Aerathir, language of the High Elves in Celestine Isles.
 - Reichsprache, spoken by the people of Kaiserreich in Drachenland.
 - Primal Tongue, ritualistic language for communications between tribes in Yuria.
 - Old Aetherian, lost language of Aetheris.
4.name: Languages

5.content:
Some of the deities of Arcanthea:
 - Asloth: Demon Queen of Spiders, Mistress of Lies and Trickery. Most influential goddess of the Dark Elves.
 - Celinestria, the Dark Maiden: Dark Elf goddess of the cosmos. Worshiped by Dark Elves who rejected Asloth.
 - Solaris: God of Dawn, Lord of Birth and Renewal. Most widely worshiped deity in Arcanthea.
 - Bahamut, the Guardian Spirit: Lord of the Sky and Dragons. High Elf's patron god.
 - Xytheris, the Shattered Whisper: Goddess of Entropy, Forgotten Memories, Chaotic Decay, and Unraveling Truths.
5.name: Deities

6.content: Solaris is the God of Dawn, Lord of Birth and Renewal. He is the most widely worshiped deity in Arcanthea. Solaris is most commonly depicted as a towering figure wreathed in golden rays of light, often as brass and gold statues.
6.name: Solaris

7.content: Asloth, the Demon Queen of Spiders, Mistress of Lies and Trickery, is the most influential goddess of the Dark Elves. Asloth is an active participant in Dark Elf society, often appearing as a beautiful but terrifying Dark Elf woman from the waist up, merging into a massive spider body. Often depicted seated on a throne made of webbing and shadows.
7.name: Asloth

8.content: Bahamut, the Guardian Spirit, Lord of the Sky and Dragons, is the High Elf's patron god. Bahamut is typically depicted as a colossal dragon with scales of pure white or silver, sometimes depicted in a more humanoid form with draconic features. Multiple unconfirmed sightings of Bahamut have been reported by multiple airship crews.
8.name: Bahamut

9.content: Celinestria, the Dark Maiden: Dark Elf goddess of the cosmos. She is depicted as an unclad Dark Elf maiden of ethereal beauty, with ankle-length silvery hair and glossy obsidian skin. Her domains include beauty, song, dance, freedom, moonlight, swordwork, and hunting.
9.name: Celinestria

10.content: Xytheris, the Shattered Whisper: Goddess of Entropy, Forgotten Memories, Chaotic Decay, and Unraveling Truths. She is depicted as a fractured silhouette, her form dissolving into moths or ash. Xytheris is neither cruel nor kind, she governs the slow death of stars, the rusting of empires, and the quiet rot beneath polished surfaces. She believes true clarity lies in disintegration. By erasing lies, illusions, and rigid narratives, she exposes raw, uncomfortable truths.
10.name: Xytheris

11.content: T'Seraa: Goddess of luck and good fortune, believed to have disappeared centuries ago, with one rumor saying she was slain by Asloth out of jealousy. She is commonly depicted as a beautiful woman with long, flowing blonde hair with platinum streaks. She had heterochromatic eyes, one eye silver, the other gold, representing different sides of the same coin. There are unconfirmed rumors of a prophecy foretelling her reincarnation into the mortal world.
11.name: T'Seraa

12.content: Orin is the larger of the two continents in Arcanthea, consisting of many kingdoms.
12.name: Orin

13.content: Drachenland is the smaller of the two continents in Arcanthea, to the west of Orin across the Sovereign Sea. Ruled entirely by Kaiserreich.
13.name: Drachenland

14.content: The Great Yuria Forest is a large woodland at the center of Orin, at the foot of the Adamantine Range. Home to Wood Elves and Ajin.
14.name: Great Yuria Forest

15.content: The Grey Marches is an arid stretch of land in southwest Orin, home to the Orcs of Rhokesh.
15.name: The Grey Marches

16.content: Adamantine Range is a long mountain range in central Orin.
16.name: Adamantine Range

17.content:
Oceans and seas of Arcanthea:
 - Sovereign Sea (between Orin and Drachenland)
 - The Glacial Expanse (northern arctic ocean)
 - Aetheric Veil (east of Orin)
 - The Endless West (west of Drachenland)
17.name: Oceans

18.content: Slavery exists with differing legality among the nations.
18.name: Slavery

19.content:
Adventurers' Guild:
 - Neutral organization with branches all over the world
 - Manages adventurers, quest assignments, member registrations, handling of rewards
 - Each Guild Hall consists of a reception area, a quest board, and a tavern
 - Adventurers are ranked from F-Rank up to S-Rank. They cannot take quests they're not qualified for
 - Each member carries their guild card, detailing their info and rank, used for taking quests and receiving rewards
19.name: Adventurers' Guild

20.content:
Adventurers' Guild Haven Branch:
 - The Guild's HQ
 - Receptionist: Willow
 - Focus: Diverse quests, merchant escorts, bounties, espionage
20.name: Adventurers' Guild - Haven

21.content:
Adventurers' Guild Genevieve Branch:
 - Receptionist: Yuri
 - Focus: Traditional adventuring, knightly quests, local legends
21.name: Adventurers' Guild - Genevieve

22.content:
Adventurers' Guild Albia Branch:
 - Receptionist: Celia
 - Focus: Maritime Monster Hunting, Cargo Recovery, Trade Escorts, "Discreet" Tasks
22.name: Adventurers' Guild - Albia

23.content:
Adventurers' Guild Altenburg Branch:
 - Receptionist: Klaus
 - Focus: Maintaining Order, Monster Hunting, Resource Acquisition
23.name: Adventurers' Guild - Altenburg

24.content:
Albia Merchants Association:
 - One of the most powerful guilds in Valoria
 - Must be registered to do any business in Albia
 - Has a banking service
 - In a constant power struggle with the VTC
24.name: Albia Merchants Association

25.content:
Royal Academy of Genevieve:
 - Most prestigious educational institution in Orin
 - Headmaster: Balthazir, a Knowledge Demon, stern but secretly kind
 - Only accepts nobles
 - Famed for its martial and magical courses
 - Students are between the age of fourteen (first-year) to twenty (seventh-year, senior)
 - Students live in dorms, and are allowed to bring along servants
 - Consists of different departments for knights, artificers, etc. The most prestigious being the Department of Battle Wizards
25.name: Royal Academy of Genevieve

26.content:
Altenburg Imperial Academy:
 - Premiere educational institution of the Empire
 - Headmaster: Baroness Helena von Degenhart, former Commander of the 8th Aegis Frame Division, oversees both traditional and AFTI programs
 - Separate classes for nobles and commoners
 - Famed for its officer and engineering courses
 - Students are between the age of sixteen (first-year) to twenty one (sixth-year, senior)
 - Noble students live in dorms with servants, commoners live in military style barracks
 - Aegis Frame Technical Institute (AFTI) is the most prestigious branch
26.name: Altenburg Imperial Academy

27.content: The Aegis Frame Technical Institute (AFTI), a specialized branch of the Altenburg Imperial Academy, trains elite Aegis Frame pilots and engineers. Admission is highly selective, open to nobles and commoners who demonstrate exceptional skill. Students follow either the Pilot Track, emphasizing combat tactics and magical theory, or the Engineer Track, focusing on mechanical systems and magical maintenance. Renowned for its rigorous training, the institute has produced many of Kaiserreich’s most distinguished Aegis Frame crews.
27.name: Aegis Frame Technical Institute

28.content: Humans are the most numerous due to the shorter lifespan and reproduction cycle. They occupy the two main continents of the planet.
28.name: Humans

29.content:
Elves, collectively known as Aelvani, are divided into sub-races:
 - High Elves (Asyran) - Fair-skinned isolationist elves who live on floating sky islands. (2000 years lifespan.)
 - Wood Elves (Sylvar) - Bronze skinned elves of shorter stature, live in glades in the Great Yuria Forest. (500 years lifespan)
 - Dark Elves (Vathren) - Darker grayish skinned elves from the archipelagos southeast of Orin. (900 years lifespan)
29.name: Elves

30.content: High Elves, or Asyran as they call themselves, reside on the Celestine Isles, a collection of magically sustained sky islands. Known for their isolationist tendencies and conservative stance toward technology, their cities are characterized by towering spires and immense, ancient trees. High Elves naturally live for around 2,000 years, though their lifespans can be significantly extended through the use of magic. They typically stand between 6'2" to 6'8" (188-203 cm) in height.
30.name: High Elves

31.content: Wood Elves, or Sylvar as they call themselves, make their home in the glades of the Great Yuria Forest, a vast woodland in central Orin. With bronze skin and a shorter, agile build, they are deeply connected to their natural surroundings. Each clan is governed by a circle of druids. Wood Elves frequently clash with poachers and slavers, defending their land fiercely. Their lifespans typically reach around 500 years, though many lives are cut short by the dangers they face. They typically stand between 5'4" to 5'10" (163-178 cm) in height.
31.name: Wood Elves

32.content: Dark Elves, or Vathren as they call themselves, are distinguished by their dark grayish skin and inhabit The Shrouds, a sprawling archipelago southeast of Orin near the equator. Their society is matriarchal, with men often facing severe oppression, and slavery is not only legal but deeply ingrained in their culture. Renowned as master sailors and feared pirates, the Dark Elves command the world's most formidable navy. Despite their potential lifespan of nearly 900 years, most meet violent ends long before reaching old age. They typically stand between 5'8" to 6'3" (173-190 cm) in height.
32.name: Dark Elves

33.content: Orcs are a dying race, with most forced into factory work or slavery. Free tribes, determined to liberate their people, made their homes in the harsh lands of the Grey Marches. Some tribes have migrated to the Arctic north pole, seeking freedom from other races. Orcs in Orin have earthy skin tones, from deep tan to reddish-brown, while Orcs in the Arctic are paler, with shades of grey, bluish-grey, or greenish undertones.
33.name: Orcs

34.content: Dwarves: They DO NOT live underground nor in the mountains, these dwarves rule the waves from colossal maritime fortresses - titanic platforms of steel and enchanted metal that float across the world's oceans. From engineering marvels like the mighty Irontide, they pioneer the fusion of magic and technology, producing most of the world's greatest inventions in partnership with their gnomish allies. These seafaring dwarves have transformed their fortified seasteads into centers of innovation, where arcane science flourishes far above the ocean floor.
34.name: Dwarves

35.content: Gnomes are integrated into dwarven society and help them with research. They are seen as equals among the dwarves.
35.name: Gnomes

36.content:
**Demi-Humans**, or Ajin as they call themselves, are humans descended from bloodlines whose ancestors had mingled with the primal forest spirits. They are humans with animal features—most commonly ears and tails from animals like cats, foxes, or wolves. They have villages and clans in the Great Yuria Forest and Adamantine Range. Ajin culture is inspired by feudal Japan.

**IMPORTANT**: Except for animal ears and tails, Ajin have otherwise human appearances. They do NOT have paws, claws, whiskers, or fur.
36.name: Ajin

37.content:
Demons are not inherently evil beings but rather ancient spirits that embody primal forces and desires. Their origins are tied to ancient deities. Unlike Primordials, demons aren't true immortals, although they don't age, they can still be permanently killed when their physical bodies are destroyed.

Cultural Views on demons:
 - High Elves: View demons as corrupted celestial spirits; officially banned but secretly consulted for knowledge.
 - Dark Elves: See demons as allies and integrate them into society, valuing their magic and wisdom.
 - Kaiserreich: Publicly bans and oppose demons but secretly employs them in organizations, including the military.
 - Vaillancourt: Regulates demon presence; requires registration and oversight but values their role in magical research and business.
 - Dwarves: Respect demons' power, maintain formal contracts, and value their expertise while distrusting their influence.
37.name: Demons

38.content:
Primordials are beings of immense power and antiquity, predating recorded history. They are the embodiment of primal forces, such as elements, emotions, or abstract concepts. Unlike common demons, they have true immortality, when their physical forms are destroyed, they dissipate into energy, returning to their primal source to reform over time, a process that can take decades to millennia.
 - Some can take the form of a mortal race
 - Their magic far exceeds mortal mages
 - Some seek power, others simply observe the world, or influence from the shadows
 - The oldest and strongest may ascend to godhood
 - Rarely seen, they are known mainly through ancient myths
 - They refer to demons as children, seeing them as lesser spirits
38.name: Primordial

39.content:
**Empire of Kaiserreich**:
 - Encompasses the continent of Drachenland, with former kingdoms as provinces
 - Renowned for its strong military and technological advancements
 - Slavery is illegal, though underground markets, especially for young elven women, persist
 - Flag: Red double-headed eagle on black with four white stars
 - Capital: Altenburg
 - Demonym: Kaiserliche, Drachenlanders (preferred by provincial lords)
39.name: Kaiserreich

40.content:
**Kingdom of Vaillancourt**: The land of magic and chivalry. A sizeable nation on the northern part of Orin. Known for its art and cuisine, and its many knightly orders. Not as technologically advanced, but do possess basic steamboats and locomotives. Slavery is only allowed for non-human races.
 - Flag: White stag with gold antlers on deep blue.
 - Ruler: King Gilles de Lumière, with Council of Elders from families of ancient mage bloodlines.
 - Capital: Genevieve
 - Locations: Village of Laval (two hours on foot from capital)
 - Borders: Frostmark (North), Rosencourt (East), Haven (South)
 - Demonym: Vaillans
40.name: Vaillancourt

41.content:
**Republic of Valoria**: 
 - Flag: Gold sea serpent on dark red
 - Ruler: Archduchess Rezalia II, and Council of Merchants
 - Capital: Albia, a grand coastal city with islands and canals
 - Location: Eastern coast of Orin
 - Known for the Valorian Trading Company (VTC), a powerful mercantile order with exclusive trade rights with Dark Elves
 - VTC has its own elite armed forces
 - Valorian Navy engages in privateering
 - Slavery is legal but frowned upon
 - Denonym: Valorians
41.name: Valoria

42.content: Irontide: The biggest of the dwarven sea fortresses, the size of a large metropolis. Democratically ruled by the elected Council of Twelve, each member considered the best engineers among the dwarves, and elections for new members only happen after one has died or disgraced. Other races are tolerated for trades, but distrusted. Slavery is only allowed for convicted criminals and prisoners of war.
42.name: Irontide

43.content:
Umbraqueth and The Shrouds:
 - Flag: Obsidian anchor entwined in webs amidst a raging storm
 - The Dark Elves’ alliance of port cities, known as the Pact of Umbraqueth, named after its largest city and de facto capital
 - Region plagued by harsh, unpredictable weather, including monsoons, tornadoes, and tsunamis
 - Developed Barrier Magic and heavy-stone, underground architecture to withstand the climate
 - Massive sea walls protect Umbraqueth’s harbor
 - Limited airships but a large ocean fleet
 - Slavery is legal and widely practiced
43.name: Umbraqueth

44.content:
**The Celestine Isles**: A High Elf sky island coalition with Starspire as its capital, ruled by an immortal soul formed from merged elven ancestor spirits.
 - Other islands, scattered worldwide, are linked by teleportation rings and ruled by High Elf Princes.
 - Entry for other races requires special permission.
 - Slavery is strictly banned; slavers are executed without exception.
 - Flag: A silver dragon atop a golden spire, flanked by towering trees.
44.name: Celestine Isles

45.content: Valandor is a floating sky island within the Celestine Isles, ruled by Prince Eldenyr Syr'alath fen'Valandor and his wife, Princess Saphielle Lun'ariel fen'Valandor. Their adopted daughter, Lunastrea Sylvanetta fen'Valandor, is a Dark Elf adventurer with a singing blade who reluctantly maintains her status as potential heir to Valandor. The island is known for the Moonspire, a central tower dedicated to celestial observation and divination.
45.name: Valandor

46.content: **Free States of Rokhesh**, in Southwestern Orin, consist of a loose confederation of Orc territories that have managed to maintain their independence. Calling themselves 'Free Orcs', the Orcs of Rokhesh are guerrilla warfare experts, using the terrain to their advantage to keep their independence. They are known for their specialized units, the "Rokhesh Rangers," gunslingers who conduct raids to free enslaved orcs.
46.name: Rokhesh

47.content: **The Confederated Tribes of Berruth**: A coalition of Orc tribes in the harsh Arctic north, beyond civilization's reach. Known as 'Ice Orcs', the Tribes of Berruth strive to preserve and rebuild pure Orcish culture, free from outside influence. They have established their territory around geothermal hot springs, which provide essential heat and power in the unforgiving cold.
47.name: Berruth

48.content: **The Principality of Rosencourt**: A tiny but wealthy nation bordered by Vaillancourt, known for luxury goods and famous for their perfumes and fashion.
48.name: Rosencourt

49.content: **Free City of Haven**: A neutral city-state, buffering Vaillancourt and Valoria.
49.name: Haven

50.content: **The Frostmark League** is an alliance of northern trading towns. Maintain good relations with the Arctic Orcs, regularly trades with them.
50.name: Frostmark

51.content: **The Sacred Valleys** is a demi-human theocratic nation in the Adamantine Range ruled by an order of monk-astronomers.
51.name: Sacred Valleys

52.content: The Snow Wolves are a proud and resilient tribe of Ajin dwelling in the harsh peaks of the Adamantine Range. Isolated from the lowlands, they have forged a unique culture deeply tied to their mountainous environment and its primal spirits. Distinguished by white or silver hair, exceptional endurance, and a strong sense of community, the tribe comprises of twelve Snow Wolf clans.
52.name: Snow Wolves

53.content: The Asuka Clan, a distinctive group of Wood Elves within the Great Yuria Forest, played a pivotal role in aiding the settlement of demi-humans in Yuria long before recorded history. Renowned for their warrior monks and philosophers, the clan is shrouded in mystery, their hidden village protected by forest spirits and accessible only to those who earn their favor. Despite their small population, members of the Asuka Clan live remarkably long lives, often doubling the lifespan of other Wood Elves. Their profound traditions have left an enduring mark on demi-human culture, shaping its very foundation.
53.name: Asuka Clan

54.content:
House Morvyth:
A rising Dark Elf power in Umbraqueth, House Morvyth has evolved from a modest trading house into a feared naval force in The Shrouds. Under Matron Mother Nezraya Tal’vestra y’th Morvyth, its influence has grown for over four centuries, backed by a formidable corsair fleet. Their insignia is a silver spider consuming a bleeding star.
Notable Figures:
 - Xylvixi: Nezraya’s niece and presumed heir
 - High Priestess Xalthara: Senior priestess of Asloth, nominal head of the Matron's Circle
 - Vex'ahlia: Commander of the House Guard (Female)
 - Echrazka: A demon serving as an information gatherer
54.name: House Morvyth

55.content:
Common Technology:
- Airships, steamboats and ironclads, firearms and artillery, steam locomotives

Advanced Technology:
- Small propeller airplanes (runic stones + steam engines), submarines (air bubbles from wind magic)

Miscellaneous Technology:
- Brass automatons as automated servants, powered by steam engines and simple runic stones, replacing mana-dependent golems
55.name: Technology

56.content: Airships are airborne ironclads, blending technology and magic in their design. Their armored hulls, reinforced with steel plating and studded with rivets, resemble naval warships. Powered by crystal engines attuned to Force or Air magic, these ships achieve vertical lift through either anti-gravity fields or wind manipulation, while horizontal propulsion relies on steam-driven propellers. Airships are used for both military and commercial applications, and scientific exploration and expeditions.
56.name: Airships

57.content:
Aegis Frames are towering humanoid combat vehicles of Kaiserreich Empire. Ranging from 12 to 20 meters, they integrate steam technology and various magics. Earth Magic strengthens frames, Force Magic optimizes weight, and crystal engines power them.
 - Only Kaiserreich possesses the expertise to build them
 - Currently in their third generation, first debuted in 1855
 - Pilots and Engineers trained at the Aegis Frame Technical Institute (AFTI)
 - Classes:
 	 - Jäger (12m): Scouts
 	 - Krieger (15m): Standard combat frames
 	 - Panzer (18m): Heavy assault
 	 - Festung (20m): Mobile command
57.name: Aegis Frames

58.content:
Jäger:
Scouts and reconnaissance class of Aegis Frames, standing at 12 meters.
Features:
 - Twin-barrel rapid-fire steam guns
 - Retractable combat knife for close encounters
 - Optional: Scout variant replaces weapons with enhanced sensor arrays
58.name: Jäger Class

59.content:
Krieger:
Standard combat Aegis Frames, standing at 15 meters.
Features:
 - Shoulder-mounted medium cannon
 - Forearm-mounted steam repeater guns
 - Power-assisted sword or polearm
 - Optional: Shield generator using Force Magic
59.name: Krieger Class

60.content:
Panzer:
Heavy assault Aegis Frames, standing at 18 meters.
Features:
 - Heavy siege cannon (main arm)
 - Multiple auxiliary gun ports
 - Pneumatic pile-driver fists
 - Reinforced armor plating with magical wards
60.name: Panzer Class

61.content:
Festung:
Mobile command & fortress Aegis Frames, standing at 20 meters.
Features:
 - Multiple defensive turrets
 - Heavy artillery platforms
 - Force Magic shield projectors
 - Rarely engages in direct combat
61.name: Festung Class

62.content:
Magic System:  
- Magic bends or breaks physical laws to achieve impossible effects  
- A mage’s aptitude and mana are predetermined by the alignment of the three moons and cannot be increased  
- Roughly 75% of the population cannot use magic, with High Elves and Dark Elves making up the majority of mages  

Disciplines:  
- Divination, Celestial, Illusion, Shadow, Force, Psionic  
- Four elemental magics: Air, Fire, Water, Earth
62.name: Magic

63.content: Divination Magic focuses on perceiving the future, past, or hidden knowledge through various methods. Accuracy varies depending on the skill of the mage and the influence of Chronos, the moon associated with time and prophecy.
63.name: Divination Magic

64.content: Celestial Magic draws power directly from the celestial bodies, especially Mystra. Encompasses spells related to healing, enhancing other magical disciplines, and creating potent wards or barriers. Power fluctuates with Mystra's orbital cycle.
64.name: Celestial Magic

65.content: Illusion Magic is the art of manipulating perception, creating phantom images, sounds, and even sensations. Ranging from simple tricks to elaborate mind-altering illusions.
65.name: Illusion Magic

66.content: Shadow Magic deals with manipulating shadows and darkness. Can be used for concealment, teleportation through shadows, or even summoning shadowy creatures. Closely associated with the moon Animus due to its connection with life, death, and spiritual energy.
66.name: Shadow Magic

67.content: Force Magic manipulates raw magical energy to create powerful concussive blasts, protective shields, or enhance physical attributes. Highly versatile and favored by warriors and those who prefer direct combat applications.
67.name: Force Magic

68.content: Psionic Magic centers on manipulating the mind and mental energies. Used for telepathy, mind control, telekinesis, and creating mental shields. Considered a dangerous discipline due to its potential for abuse.
68.name: Psionic Magic

69.content: Air Magic controls wind, weather patterns, and even flight. Can be used to create powerful gusts, tornadoes, or gentle breezes. Airships rely on a combination of Air magic and technology for propulsion and lift.
69.name: Air Magic

70.content: Fire Magic controls flames and heat. Used offensively for destructive fireballs or defensively to create walls of flame. Can also be used for more subtle applications like forging and metallurgy.
70.name: Fire Magic

71.content: Water Magic manipulates water in all its forms – ice, liquid, and vapor. Also used for transportation across bodies of water and underwater exploration.
71.name: Water Magic

72.content: Earth Magic controls earth, stone, and metal. Can be used to create earthquakes, manipulate terrain, or shape metal objects. Dwarves, renowned for their metalworking, often combine Earth magic with their technology.
72.name: Earth Magic

73.content: Rift Shifting is the process of interfering with teleportation ring's magic to divert the destination, most commonly used by slavers to kidnap High Elves.
73.name: Rift Shifting

74.content:
Succubi and Incubi:
 - Known as "Desire Weavers" in polite society
 - Naturally gifted in illusion magic and psionics, can enter people's dreams
 - Often serve as diplomats and negotiators in Dark Elf society
 - Many work as legitimate business owners in entertainment and pleasure houses
 - Some operate as information brokers and spies
 - Valued in Valorian high society as "social consultants" despite public disapproval
74.name: Succubi and Incubi

75.content:
Dragons are rare, majestic beings of immense magical power, once numerous but greatly diminished after Aetheris' fall. Key details:
 - Dragons speak only Old Aetherian fluently. Only ancient High Elves or expert scholars can understand them.
 - Dragons are sacred to High Elves, symbolizing power, wisdom, and a connection to Bahamut.
 - Dragons vary in color, size, and elemental affinity, ranging from bird-sized to larger than airships.
75.name: Dragons

76.content:
Werewolves are both feared and revered, depending on cultural perspectives:
 - The origins of lycanthropy remain mysterious, with many theories circulating.
 - Control over the wolf form varies. Some retain human intellect and can shift at will, while others lose themselves to instinct, becoming predators during full moons or high stress.
 - Some Ajin tribes revere werewolves as sacred guardians, embodying a bond with nature's spirits. They see lycanthropy as a gift and often protect werewolves as allies against threats to the forest.
 - Werewolves gain enhanced strength, speed, and senses in wolf form, making them formidable.
 - Werewolves are vulnerable to silver.
76.name: Werewolves

77.content:
Vampires:
 - Predators and dangerous outsiders who prey on the living, unlike integrated demons
 - Undead created by a dark curse, believed to stem from a corrupted ritual during Animus’s darkest phase
 - Universally feared and hunted by all nations and races
 - Numerous vampire-hunting organizations exist worldwide
 - Specialized vampire-hunting technology has been developed
 - Often infiltrate noble houses
77.name: Vampires

78.content:
The Order of the Sacred Dawn:
 - A secret order of vampire hunters within the Church of Solaris
 - Emblem: A circular crest featuring the rising sun and a flaming sword
 - Motto: Lux Vincit Tenebras
 - Operate in secrecy, unknown to most regular clergy
 - Organized into small, independent cells

Sacred Dawn Hierarchy:
 1. Grand Master – Jacques de l'Aube, the enigmatic leader known only to the Elders.
 2. Elder – Strategists and coordinators who act as the Grand Master’s agents and manage cell operations.
 3. Paladin – Elite hunters who lead individual cells in the field.
 4. Knight – Core members and experienced hunters forming the backbone of the Order.
 5. Initiate – Trainees who have yet to complete major missions.
 6. Squire – New recruits undergoing basic training.
78.name: Sacred Dawn

79.content:
The Blighted Chorus, or simply the Chorus, is an assassin order devoted to a distorted view of Xytheris. Their doctrine, the Black Verses, deems assassinations sacred. Their paradoxical name—blending decay and harmony—reflects their belief that corruption fosters unity and that violence is a transformative "song."

Core Beliefs:
 - Truth lies in dissolution
 - Memory is chains, forgetting is freedom
 - In discord we find harmony
 - Every death unwinds the world

Structure & Methods:
 - Members who lose most of their identity are believed to be closest to Xytheris
 - Leaders are the most psychologically broken
 - Accept contracts from political entities, ensuring deaths appear as divine will
 - Harsh and cruel training process, make trainees forget who they were, akin to brainwashing
 - Targets are marked with specific symbols that correspond to verses from their Black Verses
79.name: Blighted Chorus

80.content: The Rokhesh Rangers, an elite band of orc gunslingers led by Ranger General Gordakh Steeljaw, operate in autonomous teams of 4-6 to liberate enslaved orcs and defend free communities across Orin. Blending traditional warrior customs with modern guerrilla tactics, they favor customized rifles, revolvers, and lightweight armor for speed and precision. Identified by their green sashes, dyed with sacred herbs from the Grey Marches, they follow a strict code of honor while executing missions from reconnaissance to rescue. Their hidden fort, "Last Stand", remains a closely guarded secret, known only to Rangers and trusted allies.
80.name: Rokhesh Rangers

81.content:
The Midnight Raven:
The enigmatic Midnight Raven, seen as a walking calamity, is a figure shrouded in mystery. No one knows the identify of the mage who seems to have been alive for the last two centuries, playing pranks on travelers. Safiya de Noirciel's secret alias.
81.name: Midnight Raven

82.content:
**Lost Island of Aetheris**:
 - Fifteen thousand years ago, Aetheris, the most advanced High Elf sky island, fell into the ocean
 - Known as the "Crown of the Celestine Isles," its High Elves were revered as nearly divine for their magical abilities
 - Believed to lie in the Celestial Abyss, a deep trench guarded by magical anomalies and violent storms, making exploration nearly impossible
 - Rumors persist of adventurers forming expeditions to uncover the ruins
82.name: Lost Island of Aetheris

83.content: Old Aetherian is a long-lost language from the era of Aetheris, now preserved only in the inscriptions of ancient artifacts and relics. Deciphering even a single sentence often requires scholars days or even weeks of painstaking effort. Among the High Elves, only a handful of the oldest elders retain the ability to speak this ancient tongue fluently.
83.name: Old Aetherian

84.content: Notable Old Aetherian words: Syrrandri (High Elf), Sylvrana (Wood Elf), Vathrari (Dark Elf), Vith'khar (Asloth), Draekar (Dragon), Tirvalar (Human), Tharvalis (Demi-human), Noctharel (Vampire), Durazari (Dwarf), Athrani (Primordial), Y'marhazi (Demon), Kharnak'ei (Orc)
84.name: Old Aetherian Vocabulary

85.content: The Mage Wars: Happened around 100 years ago. A series of conflicts and uprisings in Orin, instigated by mages who were unhappy with their functions being replaced by technology.
85.name: The Mage Wars

86.content:
War of Three Crowns:
 - In 1702, King Laurent of Vaillancourt captured Ferremont from Rosencourt
 - Rosencourt sought to reclaim it but waited 12 years, intervening after Laurent’s death triggered a civil war between his sons, Marcel and Bernard
 - Rosencourt’s attempt led to the War of Three Crowns, ending with Marcel’s victory over both Bernard and Rosencourt
 - Marcel secured Ferremont, reunified Vaillancourt, and became King Marcel d'Étincelle
86.name: War of Three Crowns

87.content:
Kingdom of Razrias:
The prosperous Ajin kingdom of Razrias stood at the foot of what would later become the Adamantine Range. Razrias was home to twelve major clans who had united under a single banner after centuries of strife. 

The Fall of Razrias happened two millennia ago, when an unknown Primordial manifested in the kingdom's central plaza during a celestial alignment. A coalition army was formed, including neighboring human kingdoms, Elven scouts and mages, and even a contingent of Dwarven warrior. Their final stand ended in total annihilation for both the Primordial and the coalition force. The surviving Ajin bloodlines integrated into the Great Yuria Forest under Asuka Clan protection.
87.name: Kingdom of Razrias

88.content:
The VAS Tempest (Valoria Aerial Ship Tempest) is a Valorian expedition airship of medium size, lightly armed and armored compared to most airships of her size, for the purpose of speed and increased supplies for long voyages. She is very fast and agile, with a crew of twenty five. The Tempest's deadly accuracy compensates for her lighter armament. Nine state-of-the-art rifled cannons—four along each broadside and one at the bow—represent the pinnacle of modern artillery. Her crew is well-trained in long ranged marksmanship with rifles and boarding action.

Notable Tempest Crew: Lydia Neraia (Captain), Elias Sharpe (First Officer),Rowan Blackwell (Navigator), Tobias Dale (Chief Engineer), Finch (Gunner), Maia (Cook), Sienna (Lookout), Medic (Dr. Lorrain)
88.name: Tempest

89.content: Emperor Adrian von Baden Mismarck of the Empire of Kaiserreich is well-respected ruler who made his name during his youth with many notable exploits. The years had caught up to him and now he is terminally ill and bedridden.
89.name: Emperor Adrian

90.content: The Crown Prince of Kaiserreich, Lucien, is known for his good looks, spoiled upbringing, and questionable competency.
90.name: Crown Prince Lucien

91.content: Theodoric, the second prince of Kaiserreich, is known for his skills in battle and his easy-going personality. He leads the 1st Imperial Dragoons. Theodoric likes to spend his time with his men in taverns, going by simply as "Theo" to blend in with the common citizens.
91.name: Prince Theodoric

92.content: The 1st Imperial Dragoons is an elite force of hand-picked men, armed with long spears, swords, and rifles. They have a flotilla of airships, with the Steel Dragon as the flagship. Their emblem is a silver dragon head alongside a trident.
92.name: 1st Imperial Dragoons

93.content: Balthazir, current Headmaster of the Royal Academy, is a towering Knowledge Demon with obsidian skin, appointed as Headmaster in 1752. As Vaillancourt’s highest-ranking registered demon and the only non-human to lead the Academy, he commands deep respect. Though outwardly stern, he has secretly spent entire nights assisting struggling students—rumors he firmly denies to preserve his formidable reputation.
93.name: Balthazir

94.content: Helena von Degenhart, current Commandant of the Altenburg Imperial Academy, is a tall, steel-eyed woman with a distinctive white streak in her dark hair and a sophisticated mechanical right arm. This former Aegis Frame Commander lost her arm in a classified incident. Appointed in 1862, she's known for her strict adherence to imperial protocol. While publicly maintaining traditional class distinctions, she secretly implements systems to identify and promote talented commoners, working discreetly with demon artificers to advance prosthetic technology.
94.name: Helena von Degenhart

95.content: Matron Mother of House Morvyth, Nezraya Tal'vestra y'th Morvyth transformed from an overlooked daughter to a ruthless naval power's architect through calculated patience and devastating retribution. Her mastery of shadow magic and political manipulation has elevated her house over four centuries of rule. Behind her regal beauty—silver-white hair and glowing crimson eyes—lies a mind that trusts no one, having executed all who betrayed her bloodline a century after her ascension.
95.name: Nezraya

96.content: Xylvixi Nath'vari y'th Morvyth, niece to Matron Nezraya and presumed heir to House Morvyth. At 178, she is young for her race but skilled in shadow manipulation and possesses a sharp intellect that occasionally impresses her aunt. Tall and lithe, with silver-streaked obsidian hair and crimson eyes, she treads carefully—showing enough competence to remain valuable without threatening Nezraya's rule.
96.name: Xylvixi

97.content: Elise von Neuschwanstein is a 22-year-old Duchess of Neuschwanstein, orphaned at 14. Known for her intelligence and political acumen, she maintains an icy public facade but is warmer with trusted companions. Elise is engaged to Lucien, whom she dislikes. She has platinum blonde hair and pale blue eyes.
97.name: Elise

98.content: Marianne, 28 years old, is the personal hand-maid of Duchess Elise. Marianne had entered the household when she was mere 12, and had known Elise since when they were both young. She had taken care of Elise since she was 14, after the former Duke and Duchess had died.
98.name: Marianne

99.content: Yuri is the receptionist at the Genevieve branch of the Adventurer's Guild, easily recognized by her aquamarine eyes and long white hair. She is known for her polite demeanor, sharp perception, and subtle, playful humor, making her a favorite among adventurers. She is a demi-human with fox ears and tail, and otherwise human features and body.
99.name: Yuri

100.content: Willow, the newest receptionist at Haven's Adventurer's Guild, is a girl with green eyes, light brown hair, and a perpetually flustered air. She's eager to please, though often overwhelmed by the bustling HQ environment.
100.name: Willow

101.content: Celia is the receptionist at the Albia branch of the Adventurer's Guild, a tall woman with deep chestnut brown hair and green eyes. Sharp-witted, impeccably dressed, and with a keen eye for detail and a knack for negotiation, Celia commands attention from behind the reception desk, not through overt loudness, but through an aura of effortless sophistication.
101.name: Celia

102.content: Klaus is the receptionist at the Altenburg branch of the Adventurer's Guild, a stern, disciplined individual with piercing blue eyes, and a retired military officer. He is efficient, direct, and emphasize following protocol.
102.name: Klaus

103.content: Prince Eldenyr Syr'alath fen'Valandor rules Valandor with stern wisdom, his centuries of experience reflected in his silver-streaked hair and penetrating gaze. Though outwardly disapproving of his adopted daughter's adventuring, he secretly collects news of her exploits while maintaining the traditional isolationist values of High Elven society.
103.name: Eldenyr

104.content: Princess Saphielle Lun'ariel fen'Valandor, a renowned artist and healer with a gentle demeanor, serves as the emotional core of the Valandor royal family. Her studio overflows with half-finished portraits of her wayward daughter, and she maintains a collection of healing potions specifically formulated for adventuring injuries.
104.name: Saphielle

105.content: Lunastrea Sylvanetta fen'Valandor, raised among High Elves despite her Dark Elven heritage, Sylvanetta abandoned her studies at the College of Magic to become an A-Rank adventurer, wielding the singing sword Fyral Laerith. She maintains a double life as the reluctant heir to Valandor and the aspiring romance novelist 'Luna Wintershade,' while secretly searching for clues about her mysterious origins.
105.name: Sylvanetta

106.content: Cirielen Lun'ariel fen'Valandor, daughter of Prince Eldenyr and Princess Saphielle, is a precocious young High Elf with rare magical talent. She idolizes her adopted Dark Elven sister, Sylvanetta, collecting her novels and tales. Though their parents argue Sylvanetta's experience makes her the better heir, Sylvanetta insists Cirielen deserves the title. Despite this, the sisters share a deep bond, with Cirielen supporting Sylvanetta's search for her origins.
106.name: Cirielen

107.content: Bladesong: An ancient martial art of Celinestria's followers, blending elegant swordplay, arcane magic, and melodic movement. Practitioners channel cosmic energy through enchanted blades, crafting a "song" of steel and starlight. It flourished among Dark Elf sects who defied Asloth, especially those devoted to moonlight, beauty, and the blade. Most were purged during Asloth's rise, leaving true Bladesingers rare in modern Arcanthea.
107.name: Bladesong

108.content:
Astral Bladesong: Advanced form of Bladesong requiring a singing blade. Blends cosmic energy with High Elven dueling, creating visible starlight trails. Blade and wielder achieve perfect harmony, the weapon's melody responding to emotions while enhancing perception and speed.
Key Stances:
 - First Light: Opening stance focusing on defense and observation.
 - Midnight Crescent: Aggressive assault form marked by fluid, sweeping attacks.
 - Twilight: Third position emphasizing sudden strikes.
 - Celestial Arc: Aerial maneuver combining Force magic with bladework.
108.name: Astral Bladesong
